title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for topology declarations, property equivalence, policies, and ownership and requires the smallest defensible versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Topology Owner Contract · changed case · Quiz Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 8283 in a quorum queue during a planned rolling maintenance window. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
